41.sourceTitle: RIP Presta and Schrader? Schwalbe reckons its "revolutionary" new Clik valve is set to become cycling's new tyre inflation industry standard
41.evidenceText: The source discusses Schwalbe's 2024 Eurobike launch of the Clik valve but contains no data about 2026 or 2027 model year adoption by bicycle/wheelset brands.
41.extractionRationale: 2027 specifications and 2026 adoption data are not available in the provided source. The source only covers the 2024 launch announcement, not future adoption rates or brand continuity data.

45.evidenceText: DT Swiss uses their own proprietary "DT Swiss tubeless valve" system. Hunt sources mention tubeless ready rims with no reference to Clik valve. No evidence found of Clik adoption by DT Swiss, Mavic, Hunt, Zipp, ENVE, or Roval in available sources.
45.extractionRationale: Based on the three provided sources, there is no evidence that any major wheelset brands have adopted Clik valve as standard. DT Swiss explicitly promotes their own proprietary valve system. Hunt's tubeless wheel information contains no mention of Clik valve. The sources do not contain information about 2026 or 2027 product lines adopting Clik valve, therefore the percentage of major brands converted is 0%.

57.evidenceText: Sources show conflicting availability status: Source 1 states "We are working hard to make the product available again" (out of stock), but no specific dates provided. Source 2 (German market) shows product listed normally without stock warnings. Source 3 shows product as available. No specific 'back in stock' dates found to calculate turnaround time.
57.extractionRationale: Cannot determine B2B restock turnaround time because: (1) No specific dates are provided for when the product went out of stock or when it's expected back, (2) Evidence shows conflicting availability status across sources, (3) No calculable gap between out-of-stock and back-in-stock dates is mentioned. The German market source (most relevant) does not clearly indicate current out-of-stock status.
